customer_anxiety_profile: No anxiety signals — Kyle is a warm, self-motivated lead. His posture is curious and research-oriented ('I don't really have a basis for what it would cost, what it would get me'). The risk is not resistance; it's a two-week cooling-off window and a missing decision-maker.
unresolved_concerns: Wife (title holder) was not confirmed as a participant in the appointment — closer should verify she will be present before the call begins
Kyle's expectation may be an in-person visit ('stop by' language used by rep) — closer should clarify this is a phone appointment at the start of the call
No verbal handshake ('is that fair') was obtained — appointment confirmation is softer than protocol requires

Large home (6,000-7,000 sq ft) with high usage (4,000 kWh/month, $500-600/month to Duke) — bill pain was never emotionally activated by the setter; closer has a clean opportunity to flip it

closer_should_know: The wife is the title holder and was not confirmed as a participant — verify she is on the call before proceeding. Kyle's stated motivation is charging his new Tesla from solar; the Powerwall is the product that directly answers his logic and was never named by the setter. The bill ($500-600/month, 4,000 kWh/month with Duke, seasonal peaks) was captured but never emotionally activated — the closer has a clean first-move opportunity to flip it.
